OST_TRE_TAL.name: Tall Tree

supporting_papers.citation: Dong, R., Zhang, Y., & Zhao, J. (2018). How Green Are the Streets Within the Sixth Ring Road of Beijing? An Analysis Based on Tencent Street View Pictures and the Green View Index. International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health, 15(7), 1367.

OST_TRE_TAL.definition: Trees of significant height used to provide visual mass in wide spaces.
OST_TRE_TAL.first_confirmed_by: Dong, R., Zhang, Y., & Zhao, J. (2018). How Green Are the Streets Within the Sixth Ring Road of Beijing? An Analysis Based on Tencent Street View Pictures and the Green View Index. International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health, 15(7), 1367.

OST_TRE.name: Tree
OST_TRE.definition: A perennial woody plant characterized by an elongated stem, or trunk, supporting branches and leaves. In the context of remote sensing and semantic segmentation, this class encompasses all standard tree vegetation distinguishable from shrubs or ground cover, serving as a critical environmental feature for urban canopy analysis, carbon stock estimation, and visual occlusion detection in mapping datasets.

supporting_papers.citation: Chen, J., Zhou, C., & Li, F. (2020). Quantifying the green view indicator for assessing urban greening quality: An analysis based on Internet-crawling street view data. Ecological Indicators, 113, 106192.

Gao, F., Li, S., Tan, Z., Zhang, X., Lai, Z., & Tan, Z. (2021). How Is Urban Greenness Spatially Associated with Dockless Bike Sharing Usage on Weekdays, Weekends, and Holidays? ISPRS International Journal of Geo-Information, 10(4), 238.
Gao, L., Xiang, X., Chen, W., Nong, R., Zhang, Q., Chen, X., & Chen, Y. (2024). Research on Urban Street Spatial Quality Based on Street View Image Segmentation. Sustainability, 16(16), 7184.

Jing, F., Liu, L., Zhou, S., Song, J., Wang, L., Zhou, H., Wang, Y., & Ma, R. (2021). Assessing the Impact of Street-View Greenery on Fear of Neighborhood Crime in Guangzhou, China. International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health, 18(1), 311.

Meng, Y., Xing, H., Yuan, Y., Wong, M. S., & Fan, K. (2020). Sensing urban poverty: From the perspective of human perception-based greenery and open-space landscapes. Computers, Environment and Urban Systems, 84, 101544.

O'Regan, A. C., Byrne, R., Hellebust, S., & Nyhan, M. M. (2022). Associations between Google Street View-derived urban greenspace metrics and air pollution measured using a distributed sensor network. Sustainable Cities and Society, 87, 104221.

OST_TRE_CMP.name: Tree Complex (or Clump)
OST_TRE_CMP.definition: A vegetative cluster consisting of multiple trees growing in such close proximity that their individual crowns merge, making the isolation of single stems computationally difficult. This classification is used in segmentation tasks to denote an aggregate biological mass that functions as a single continuous obstacle or visual block, distinct from isolated specimen trees.

supporting_papers.citation: Hao, N., Li, X., Han, D., & Nie, W. (2024). Quantifying the Impact of Street Greening during Full-Leaf Seasons on Emotional Perception: Guidelines for Resident Well-Being. Forests, 15(1), 119.

OST_TRE_LCR.name: Tree (Live Crown Ratio / Low Crown)
OST_TRE_LCR.definition: A subtype classification referring to trees defined by their specific canopy structure, often relating to the Live Crown Ratio (the proportion of total tree height supported by living foliage). In urban perception contexts, this typically identifies trees with low-hanging branches or canopies that extend significantly down the trunk, potentially obstructing ground-level visibility or interfering with pedestrian and vehicular vertical clearance.

supporting_papers.citation: Dong, R., Zhang, Y., & Zhao, J. (2018). How Green Are the Streets Within the Sixth Ring Road of Beijing? An Analysis Based on Tencent Street View Pictures and the Green View Index. International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health, 15(7), 1367.

OST_UNK.name: Unknown
OST_UNK.definition: A classification assigned to data points, pixels, or regions where the feature cannot be confidently identified due to occlusion, low resolution, sensor noise, or visual ambiguity. This label is critical in quality control and uncertainty modeling, indicating areas where the sensor data or visual evidence is insufficient for definitive semantic categorization.

supporting_papers.citation: Chan, T.-C., Lee, P.-H., Lee, Y.-T., & Tang, J.-H. (2024). Exploring the spatial association between the distribution of temperature and urban morphology with green view index. PLOS ONE, 19(5), e0301921.

OST_UNSPECIFIED.name: Unspecified
OST_UNSPECIFIED.definition: A label applied to valid entities that have been detected as objects but have not been assigned a specific attribute or subtype within the annotation schema. Unlike 'Unknown' (which implies identification failure), this category suggests the object is recognized as a valid entity but lacks the necessary metadata or distinct features required for deeper taxonomical sorting.

supporting_papers.citation: Li, X., Ratti, C., & Seiferling, I. (2017). Mapping Urban Landscapes Along Streets Using Google Street View. Lecture Notes in Geoinformation and Cartography, 341–356.

Li, X., Ratti, C., & Seiferling, I. (2018). Quantifying the shade provision of street trees in urban landscape: A case study in Boston, USA, using Google Street View. Landscape and Urban Planning, 169, 81–91.

OST_FNC.name: Fence
OST_FNC.definition: Designates a freestanding structure designed to restrict or prevent movement across a boundary while often maintaining some degree of visual permeability. Unlike solid walls, fences are typically constructed from posts, wire, wood, or metal rails. In spatial coding, fences mark territorial limits or safety zones without necessarily blocking airflow or sightlines completely. This distinction is vital for studies involving ventilation, surveillance, or visual connectivity, as fences define space psychologically and legally without the mass and opacity of masonry walls.

supporting_papers.citation: Tao, Y., Wang, Y., Wang, X., Tian, G., & Zhang, S. (2022). Measuring the Correlation between Human Activity Density and Streetscape Perceptions: An Analysis Based on Baidu Street View Images in Zhengzhou, China. Land, 11(3), 400.

OST_FNC.first_confirmed_by: Chen, M., Cai, Y., Guo, S., Sun, R., Song, Y., & Shen, X. (2024). Evaluating implied urban nature vitality in San Francisco: An interdisciplinary approach combining census data, street view images, and social media analysis. Urban Forestry & Urban Greening, 95, 128289.

OST_IND_GRN.name: Individual Greenery
OST_IND_GRN.definition: Distinct, standalone vegetation elements, primarily single trees or large bushes, that can be individually segmented and counted. Unlike continuous road greenery or dense forest patches, these act as discrete vertical objects within the urban landscape model, often requiring individual maintenance or collision modeling.

supporting_papers.citation: Hu, A., Yabuki, N., Fukuda, T., Kaga, H., Takeda, S., & Matsuo, K. (2023). Harnessing multiple data sources and emerging technologies for comprehensive urban green space evaluation. Cities, 143, 104562.

OST_LGT.name: Street Lighting / Luminaire
OST_LGT.definition: Infrastructure specifically designed and installed for the artificial illumination of public outdoor spaces. This class encompasses street lamps, lantern posts, wall-mounted exterior fixtures, and floodlights, which are critical for nighttime visibility, safety, and the aesthetic definition of the urban environment.

supporting_papers.citation: Larkin, A., Gu, X., Chen, L., & Hystad, P. (2021). Predicting perceptions of the built environment using GIS, satellite and street view image approaches. Landscape and Urban Planning, 216, 104257.

OST_SGN.name: Sign
OST_SGN.definition: Subtype concept "Sign". Used in evidence/iom records to categorize subtype-level attributes; auto-generated definition pending paper-specific elaboration.

supporting_papers.citation: Gao, L., Xiang, X., Chen, W., Nong, R., Zhang, Q., Chen, X., & Chen, Y. (2024). Research on Urban Street Spatial Quality Based on Street View Image Segmentation. Sustainability, 16(16), 7184.

OST_SGN.first_confirmed_by: Cai, M., Xiang, L., & Ng, E. (2023). How does the visual environment influence pedestrian physiological stress? Evidence from high-density cities using ambulatory technology and spatial machine learning. Sustainable Cities and Society, 96, 104695.

OST_SHR.name: Shrub
OST_SHR.definition: Refers to woody plants smaller than trees, typically having several main stems arising at or near the ground. In the context of spatial analysis or environmental modeling, this category includes bushes and hedges used for landscaping, screening, or ecological diversity. These elements affect microclimates by providing low-level windbreaks and shading ground surfaces, though less effectively than canopy trees. They are distinct from ground cover due to their height and structure, playing a crucial role in the visual stratification of green spaces.

supporting_papers.citation: Tong, M., She, J., Tan, J., Li, M., Ge, R., & Gao, Y. (2020). Evaluating Street Greenery by Multiple Indicators Using Street-Level Imagery and Satellite Images: A Case Study in Nanjing, China. Forests, 11(12), 1347.
Larkin, A., & Hystad, P. (2019). Evaluating street view exposure measures of visible green space for health research. Journal of Exposure Science & Environmental Epidemiology, 29(4), 447–456.

OST_SHR.first_confirmed_by: Zhao, X., & Lin, G. (2024). Research on the Perception Evaluation of Urban Green Spaces Using Panoramic Images and Deep Learning: A Case Study of Zhujiang Park in Guangzhou. Landscape Architecture Frontiers, 12(6), 007-024.

OST_TMP.name: Temporary Object
OST_TMP.definition: Transient items or structures present in the environment that are not permanent fixtures. This category includes construction barriers, scaffolding, traffic cones, mobile vendor stalls, and debris. These objects are expected to move or disappear over time and are often filtered out for long-term mapping applications.

supporting_papers.citation: Hu, A., Yabuki, N., Fukuda, T., Kaga, H., Takeda, S., & Matsuo, K. (2023). Harnessing multiple data sources and emerging technologies for comprehensive urban green space evaluation. Cities, 143, 104562.

OST_TRE_CAN.name: Tree Canopy
OST_TRE_CAN.definition: The upper layer or habitat zone formed by the mature crowns of trees. In urban object classification (`H_objects`), the canopy is often segmented separately from the trunk (`OBJ_TRU`). This term encompasses the branches, twigs, and leaves. Analyzing the tree canopy is essential for urban climatology (measuring shade and cooling effects), air quality assessment, and determining the 'Sky View Factor' in street canyons.

supporting_papers.citation: Sakamoto, S., Kogure, M., Hanibuchi, T., Nakaya, N., Hozawa, A., & Nakaya, T. (2023). Effects of greenery at different heights in neighbourhood streetscapes on leisure walking: a cross-sectional study using machine learning of streetscape images in Sendai City, Japan. International Journal of Health Geographics, 22, 29.

OST_VEG_CLM.name: Climbing Plant
OST_VEG_CLM.definition: Vegetation that climbs or trails along vertical or sloped supports, such as vines on walls, pergolas, trellises, or building facades.

OST_GCV.name: Ground Cover
OST_GCV.definition: Low-growing vegetation layer covering the ground surface, including herbaceous planting, low creepers, or other continuous vegetated surface treatments distinct from shrubs and tree canopy.

OST_GRS.name: Grass
OST_GRS.definition: Grass or lawn vegetation forming a low horizontal green layer on the ground plane.

OST_FCD.name: Facade
OST_FCD.definition: Building facade or vertical exterior surface that frames or encloses space and may act as a carrier for visual or vegetative interventions.

OST_WAL.name: Wall
OST_WAL.definition: Wall or solid vertical boundary surface distinct from a fence, often with high opacity and stronger effects on enclosure, visibility, and spatial definition.

OST_ROD.name: Road
OST_ROD.definition: Roadway or vehicular carriageway forming the primary motorized movement surface in the streetscape.

OST_SDW.name: Sidewalk
OST_SDW.definition: Sidewalk, footpath, or pedestrian pavement forming the primary walking surface along a street or public space.

OST_FUR.name: Street Furniture
OST_FUR.definition: Street furniture or site furnishings such as benches, seating, bollards, planters, bins, and related public-space fixtures.

OST_OVH.name: Overhead Structure
OST_OVH.definition: Built overhead element such as awning, pergola, arcade, eave, canopy, bridge, or similar elevated cover/support that affects enclosure, shade, and foreground obstruction.

OST_WTR_FTN.name: Fountain
OST_WTR_FTN.definition: Designed water feature characterized by jets, sprays, basins, or ornamental circulating water displays.

OST_WTR_PND.name: Pond
OST_WTR_PND.definition: Still or slow-moving standing water body, natural or artificial, visible as a discrete water surface within the scene.

OST_WTR_STR.name: Stream
OST_WTR_STR.definition: Linear or flowing surface-water feature such as a stream, channel, or runnel visible within the scene.

OST_WTR_LAK.name: Lake
OST_WTR_LAK.definition: Large open water body or lake-like visible water surface forming a dominant blue-space element.

OST_TRN.name: Terrain / Landform
OST_TRN.definition: Ground-form element such as slope, berm, mound, or terrain surface whose shaping or materiality influences the visual field.

OST_HSC.name: Hardscape
OST_HSC.definition: Non-vegetated paved or engineered ground surface including plazas, depaved areas, impervious surfaces, and similar hard landscape material.

OST_BLT_MAS.name: Building Massing
OST_BLT_MAS.definition: Built-form massing condition including building bulk, height, setback, or enclosure attributes that structure the street canyon or scene frame.

KPN_CFA.name: Humid Subtropical (Cfa)

KPN_CFB.name: Oceanic (Cfb)
KPN_CFB.definition: Under the Köppen climate classification, the Cfb designation represents an Oceanic climate. It is characterized by temperate weather patterns featuring warm, but not hot, summers where the average temperature of the warmest month remains below 22°C. Winters are generally mild, rarely experiencing extreme freezing conditions for extended periods. Precipitation is dispersed relatively evenly throughout the year without a distinct dry season. This climate is typically found on the western sides of continents at middle latitudes, where maritime air masses heavily influence and moderate temperature extremes.

KPN_DFA.name: Hot-summer Humid Continental (Dfa)
KPN_DFA.definition: The Dfa classification signifies a Hot-summer Humid Continental climate. This zone experiences distinct seasonal variations with hot summers, where the average temperature in the warmest month exceeds 22°C, and cold winters with average temperatures dropping significantly below freezing. Precipitation is generally well-distributed year-round, though some locations may see a summer peak. Common in the interior regions of continents in the Northern Hemisphere, this climate poses specific challenges for urban planning, requiring energy-intensive cooling in summer and substantial heating infrastructure during the harsh winter months.

KPN_DWA.name: Monsoon Humid Continental (Dwa)
KPN_DWA.definition: The Dwa classification describes a Monsoon-influenced Hot-summer Humid Continental climate. It is distinguished by a strong seasonal pattern featuring very cold, dry winters and hot, wet summers. The average temperature of the warmest month is above 22°C, while the coldest month falls below freezing. The defining characteristic is the pronounced winter drought caused by the Asian winter monsoon (high pressure systems), contrasting sharply with heavy summer rainfall. This climate requires urban design strategies that can handle intense summer humidity and precipitation while mitigating dry, freezing winter conditions.

KPN_AF.name: Köppen Climate: Tropical Rainforest (Af)
KPN_AF.definition: A climate classification characterized by constant high temperatures and abundant precipitation throughout the year. Under the Köppen system (Af), every month has an average precipitation of at least 60 mm, meaning there is no distinct dry season. These conditions, typically found within 5 to 10 degrees of the equator, support dense vegetation, rapid biomass growth, and high biodiversity.

KPN_XX.name: Unknown or Undefined Climate
KPN_XX.definition: A placeholder designation used in geospatial datasets when the climate classification for a specific region cannot be determined. This typically occurs due to missing meteorological data, insufficient observation stations, or processing errors that prevent the application of standard Köppen-Geiger criteria to the location.

KPN_CSB.name: Warm-Summer Mediterranean Climate
KPN_CSB.definition: A temperate climate subtype designated as 'Csb' in the Köppen-Geiger system. It is characterized by dry, warm summers and mild, wet winters. Unlike hot-summer Mediterranean climates, the warmest month has an average temperature below 22°C (72°F), but at least four months average above 10°C. It is commonly found in coastal regions such as the Pacific Northwest, Portugal, and parts of Chile.

KPN_DFB.name: Warm-Summer Humid Continental Climate
KPN_DFB.definition: A continental climate ('Dfb') defined by large seasonal temperature variances with no significant dry season. It features warm, humid summers (warmest month below 22°C) and freezing winters (coldest month below -3°C or 0°C). This climate is common in Eastern Europe, parts of Scandinavia, and the northern United States.

KPN_CWA.name: Monsoon-Influenced Humid Subtropical Climate
KPN_CWA.definition: A temperate climate ('Cwa') characterized by hot, humid summers and mild, dry winters. The distinctive feature is a strong seasonal precipitation pattern driven by monsoons, where the wettest summer month receives at least ten times the rainfall of the driest winter month. The warmest month averages above 22°C.

KPN_NA.definition: A data flag indicating that climate classification is not relevant for the specific coordinate or area. This is most commonly applied to bodies of water (oceans, large lakes) or permanent ice sheets (Antarctica, Greenland) where vegetation-based climate definitions used in the Köppen-Geiger system are not applicable.

KPN_AM.name: Tropical Monsoon (Köppen Am)
KPN_AM.definition: Tropical monsoon climate: short dry season with high annual rainfall; e.g., Mumbai, Yangon, Colombo.

LCZ_1.name: LCZ 1 - Compact High-rise
LCZ_1.definition: Local Climate Zone 1 consists of a dense arrangement of tall buildings, typically rising ten stories or higher. The built environment is characterized by minimal spacing between structures, leading to a high aspect ratio that significantly reduces sky view factors. The ground surface is predominantly paved with concrete, asphalt, or other impervious materials, leaving little room for vegetation or trees. This zone creates intense urban heat island effects due to substantial thermal mass, reduced ventilation, and the trapping of radiative heat within the deep street canyons.

LCZ_2.name: LCZ 2 - Compact Mid-rise
LCZ_2.definition: Local Climate Zone 2 is defined by a dense mix of mid-rise structures, generally ranging from three to nine stories in height. Buildings are closely spaced, resulting in limited sky visibility and moderate-to-high canyon geometries. The land cover is dominated by impervious surfaces such as stone, brick, tile, and concrete, with few or no trees present. The thermal behavior of this zone involves significant heat storage during the day and slow release at night, contributing noticeably to the urban heat island effect, though typically with slightly less intensity than high-rise compact zones.

LCZ_3.name: LCZ 3 - Compact Low-rise
LCZ_3.definition: Local Climate Zone 3 features a dense collection of low-rise buildings, typically one to three stories tall. These structures are often attached or separated by very narrow alleys, maximizing building coverage within the lot. The surrounding ground surface is primarily paved with hard materials, offering minimal pervious land cover or vegetation. Due to the high density of small structures and lack of greenery, this zone experiences reduced airflow and significant thermal retention. It is representative of historic city centers, dense residential suburbs, or informal settlements where land use is extremely intensive.

LCZ_4.name: LCZ 4 - Open High-rise
LCZ_4.definition: Local Climate Zone 4 is characterized by an open arrangement of tall buildings, generally exceeding ten stories. Unlike compact zones, the buildings here are set significantly apart, allowing for ample airflow and sky visibility. The space between the structures is dominated by low plants, scattered trees, or grass, rather than continuous pavement. This configuration typically creates a cooler microclimate compared to compact zones because the vegetation aids in evapotranspiration and the spacing prevents heat trapping, although the tall structures can still influence wind patterns and cast significant shadows.

LCZ_6.name: LCZ 6 - Open Low-rise
LCZ_6.definition: Local Climate Zone 6 consists of a sparse arrangement of low-rise buildings, typically one to three stories in height. The buildings are detached and separated by generous amounts of permeable land cover, such as lawns, gardens, or scattered trees. This zone represents typical suburban residential areas where hardscape is minimal compared to the abundance of low vegetation. The open spacing allows for good ventilation and creates a thermal environment that is generally cooler and more responsive to diurnal temperature cycles than compact urban zones.

LCZ_A.name: LCZ A - Dense Trees
LCZ_A.definition: Local Climate Zone A describes a natural environment heavily populated with deciduous or coniferous trees. The distinguishing feature is a continuous or near-continuous canopy that significantly obstructs the view of the sky and shades the ground. The surface layer consists of permeable soil, leaf litter, or low herbaceous plants. This zone provides maximum evapotranspirative cooling and shading, maintaining lower surface and air temperatures compared to built environments. It includes forests, dense woodlots, or mature urban parks where the density of trees dominates the land cover.

LCZ_B.name: LCZ B - Scattered Trees
LCZ_B.definition: Local Climate Zone B represents a landscape featuring a sparse arrangement of trees set against a background of low plant cover, such as grass or shrubs. The tree canopy is open, allowing sunlight to reach the permeable soil surface freely. This zone is typical of parklands, savannas, or agricultural estates with scattered orchards. While it offers some shading and cooling benefits through evapotranspiration, the thermal regulation is less intense than in dense forests (LCZ A) due to increased solar exposure on the ground surface.

LCZ_UNSPECIFIED.name: Unspecified Local Climate Zone
LCZ_UNSPECIFIED.definition: A category used when available land use or morphological data is insufficient to assign a specific Local Climate Zone (LCZ 1-10 or A-G). This designation indicates that properties such as building height, sky view factor, or surface cover fraction could not be determined with enough confidence to meet the Stewart & Oke classification standards.

LCZ_URB.name: Urban / Built-up Area
LCZ_URB.definition: A generalized aggregation of Local Climate Zones 1 through 10. This term distinguishes broadly defined 'built' environments—dominated by human-made structures and impervious surfaces—from 'natural' land cover types (LCZ A-G). It is often used in simplified urban heat island studies to contrast city areas against rural surroundings.

AGE_ADL.definition: A demographic cohort representing the primary working-age population. While specific statistical definitions vary by country, this group typically encompasses individuals aged 15 to 64 (or 18 to 64). This segment acts as the economic base of a population, distinct from dependent groups such as children and the elderly.

AGE_UNSPECIFIED.definition: A data category for records where the age of the individual is unknown, missing, or withheld. AGE_ALL.definition: An aggregate demographic category representing the total population across the entire spectrum of age ranges. This term is typically used as a baseline or denominator in statistical calculations to determine the prevalence or distribution of specific variables within the general population.

ARC_FRM.name: Framed scenery archetype
ARC_FRM.definition: A scenic view characterized by a salient foreground frame that encloses or emphasizes the central focal scene (often with relatively stable left-right symmetry).

supporting_papers.citation: Lan, J., Liu, M., Luiten, E., Bracken, G., & Zhang, Q. (2025). Exploring spatial visual characteristics of scenic archetypes through AI multimodal mapping methods in Hangzhou Westlake. npj Heritage Science, 13, 658.

ARC_OBS.name: Obstructive scenery archetype
ARC_OBS.definition: A scenic view characterized by prominent foreground elements that partially block the view and create layered occlusion, producing a clear near-field dominance and visual interruption.

supporting_papers.citation: Lan, J., Liu, M., Luiten, E., Bracken, G., & Zhang, Q. (2025). Exploring spatial visual characteristics of scenic archetypes through AI multimodal mapping methods in Hangzhou Westlake. npj Heritage Science, 13, 658.

ARC_PRV.name: Porous scenery archetype
ARC_PRV.definition: A scenic view characterized by semi-transparent or perforated foreground elements that allow partial visibility of the middle/background, producing a permeable transition between layers.

supporting_papers.citation: Lan, J., Liu, M., Luiten, E., Bracken, G., & Zhang, Q. (2025). Exploring spatial visual characteristics of scenic archetypes through AI multimodal mapping methods in Hangzhou Westlake. npj Heritage Science, 13, 658.

ARC_SND.name: Sandwiched scenery archetype
ARC_SND.definition: A scenic view characterized by a middle/background focal scene being visually enclosed or bracketed by strong foreground/midground elements on multiple sides, creating a “sandwiched” depth structure.

supporting_papers.citation: Lan, J., Liu, M., Luiten, E., Bracken, G., & Zhang, Q. (2025). Exploring spatial visual characteristics of scenic archetypes through AI multimodal mapping methods in Hangzhou Westlake. npj Heritage Science, 13, 658.

patch_notes: Standardized missing/empty fields across Evidence by ensuring all records share the same top-level and nested keys (without expanding free-form dicts such as indicator.variables).
Filled missing scalar values with project missing-value encodings (e.g., NUM_NA/TXT_NA). For structural fields, used empty containers where appropriate (e.g., indicator.variables={}).
Added is_descriptive_statistic and descriptive_content to all Evidence records (default False / TXT_NA).
Replaced any 'N/A' magnitude values in IOM with NUM_NA and harmonized non-numeric magnitude values to categorical unit UNT_CAT.

Documented controlled values for Evidence.relationship.effect_size.type (r, R2, beta, B) in Appendix:D_effect_size_types and added notes to relevant B_units to prevent ambiguity between R² and signed effect sizes.
Added Context.urban_form.schema_note to document the conceptual separation and coding rule between lcz_type_id (LCZ) and space_type_id (setting).
P2 patch: added controlled role codes under Z_abbreviations.roles and added role_code to all IOM signatures while preserving the original role text.
P2 patch: added controlled scope-pattern codes under Z_abbreviations.scope_patterns and added pattern_code to IOM.operation.scope where a pattern is present.
P2 patch: expanded H_subtypes with a small normalization layer for overhead structures, water-feature subtypes, terrain, hardscape, and building massing.
P2 patch: completed subtype normalization for all remaining IOM signatures by assigning subtype_code or subtype_codes while preserving the original subtype text.
P2 audit completion: promoted all manually reviewed referenced provisional dictionary entries to confirmed, retained audit traces, and left previously deprecated unreferenced entries unchanged.
P2 signature standardization: normalized all IOM.operation.signatures subtype fields to subtype_codes (array form).
P2 signature standardization: added sig_instance_id to every IOM signature and clarified in metadata that sig_id is a reusable signature template/class identifier rather than an instance-unique key.
2026-03-19 patch: canonicalized signature naming metadata to SIG_{ACT}_{OBJ}_{TIER}_{VAR} and added rendered-axis examples.
2026-03-19 patch: added indicator-level operational_alignment blocks to distinguish representational scope from admissible operational levers observed in IOM records.
2026-03-19 patch: added setting hierarchy parents for SET_RES and SET_HISTORIC_URBAN_CORE and documented exact-or-parent-child compatibility checks.
2026-03-19 patch: corrected IND_PCI semantic_layer_scope from OBJ_VEG to OBJ_BLT and propagated synchronized matrix refs to Evidence and IOM.

2026-03-19 patch: downgraded invalid SCP_S3 records without phases to SCP_S2; sanitized narrative placeholders in Evidence/IOM/Context; split mixed/incompatible Context records by evidence setting where needed; added explicit Z_signatures activation count fields.
[2026-05-02] Joined supporting_papers from SVCs_P_Evidence and Transferability_Context into Encoding_Dictionary; pruned orphan codes without theoretical or evidence backing.

field_guidelines.Context.urban_form.lcz_type_id: Morphology/land-cover classification at neighborhood scale using Local Climate Zone codes from Appendix.L_lcz.
field_guidelines.Context.urban_form.space_type_id: Functional/setting classification of the studied space using setting codes from Appendix.E_settings.
field_guidelines.Context.urban_form.coding_rule: Treat lcz_type_id and space_type_id as complementary axes; do not infer one from the other unless explicitly stated or justified in applicability.conditions.
field_guidelines.Context.urban_form.schema_note: Stored centrally in Encoding_Dictionary_P2.metadata.field_guidelines; Context records use lcz_type_id and space_type_id directly and do not repeat a per-record schema_note.
field_guidelines.IOM.operation.signatures.sig_id: Template/class identifier for an operation signature. sig_id may repeat within the same IOM record when multiple signature instances share the same template but differ in subtype_codes, role_code, mechanism, or phase.
field_guidelines.IOM.operation.signatures.sig_instance_id: Unique instance identifier for a signature within an IOM record. Generated deterministically as {iom_id}__SIG_XX and intended for machine parsing, validation, and UI referencing.
field_guidelines.IOM.operation.signatures.subtype_codes: Normalized machine-readable subtype field for IOM signatures. Always stored as an array of subtype codes from H_subtypes, including single-subtype cases.
field_guidelines.IOM.operation.signatures.normalization_r: Use subtype_codes only; do not mix subtype_code and subtype_codes in the same dataset version. Keep subtype as a human-readable text label and use subtype_codes for computation and validation.
field_guidelines.A_indicators.operational_alignment: Structured bridge between indicator-level representational scope and the admissible operational scope observed in IOM signatures. Use this block to distinguish how an indicator is defined from how interventions may act on it.
field_guidelines.A_indicators.operational_alignment.repre: Copied from A_indicators[*].svc_matrix_expansion and treated as the canonical indicator-level representational scope.
field_guidelines.A_indicators.operational_alignment.admis: Union of signature axes observed across linked IOM records for the indicator. May extend beyond representational scope when the intervention works indirectly.
field_guidelines.Context.validation.setting_compatibility: Exact or parent-child compatibility check between Context.urban_form.space_type_id and linked Evidence.study_design.setting_type_id.
field_guidelines.IOM.operation.scope.signature_count: Number of signatures in the IOM.operation.signatures array.
field_guidelines.IOM.operation.scope.phase_count: Number of distinct non-null phase values across signatures.
field_guidelines.IOM.operation.scope.scope_validation_sta: valid when scope_id matches signature_count/phase_count rules; invalid otherwise.

performance_dimension_merge.note: Current live top-level performance scheme is the six-dimension framework defined in C_performance and used by the Evidence and IOM records. Legacy top-level codes are not part of the active dimension scheme.

metadata.p2_scope: Normalize remaining subtype / role / pattern fields without changing IDs or link structure.

provisional_audit.note: All referenced provisional entries used by current P2 records were manually audited and promoted to confirmed. Previously deprecated unreferenced provisional entries were left unchanged.

p2_repair_patch.note: Patch applies the final adopted repair list: direction semantic correction, descriptive evidence propagation, metadata synchronization, and provisional-entry usage audit.

p2_provisional_audit_completion.note: This patch finalizes the provisional-entry audit by updating status fields only, preserving all code values and cross-file links.

fix_changelog_P2_FINAL_v2: FIX 1: Z_signatures.activated_by_iom — removed 63 duplicate entries, removed 4 dangling refs: ['I_SVCs_Chen2020_2', 'I_SVCs_Jing2024_2', 'I_SVCs_Liu2023_2', 'I_SVCs_Zheng2024_3']
FIX 2: C_performance evidence_count — synchronized to the current live six-dimension scheme.
FIX 3: A_indicators — added usage_status field: 64 active, 16 reserved
FIX 4: Reclassified 16 contexts from multi_case to single_case
FIX 3b: Deleted 16 reserved indicators from A_indicators; fixed 1 narrative reference to IND_SAF_IDX in I_SVCs_Zhang2024_9; removed usage_status field (all 64 remaining indicators are active)
metadata.archetype_audit_note: Archetype field audited 102 CTX records. All remain ARC_NA because the defined archetypes (ARC_FRM, ARC_OBS, ARC_PRV, ARC_SND from Lan et al., 2025) describe specific scenic visual composition patterns that none of the 102 source studies explicitly analyze. 13 park/scenic/greenway CTX records (CTX_Chen2023_1, CTX_Cheng2024_1, CTX_Huang2022_1, CTX_Meng2024_1, CTX_Qin2024_1...) are potential candidates for future archetype annotation if original imagery is re-examined.
metadata.signature_axes_order: Operation × Semantic Layer × Spatial Layer × Morphological Layer

revision_summary_2026_03_17.goal: Align the four JSON files with Section 2.1–2.3 of the I–SVCs–P framework.
changes: Mapped the seven-dimension operational scheme to the six framework dimensions.
Reordered signature IDs to Operation × Semantic × Spatial × Morphological per Section 2.2.
Added explicit Section 2.2 layer dictionaries and indicator-level N × 3 × K matrix expansion fields.

metadata.context_linkage_note: Context records remain study-level applicability envelopes linked to evidence/IOM records; this revision preserves that structure while making the framework correspondence explicit.

clean_delivery_patch_2026_03_17.note: Clean delivery patch for v5_3: synchronized metadata to the active six-dimension framework and removed outdated live-scheme references.

setting_compatibility_rule_2026_03_19.note: Use hierarchy compatibility rather than strict string equality when Context is more specific than Evidence or vice versa.

problem4_source_cleanup_20260319.scope: Remove source-cluster contamination identified for Song2023 and Wang2024 DOI groups using the original PDFs as source-of-truth.

count_field_interpretation_20260319.note: Legacy codes_total_before/codes_total_after are retained as historical pruning-event metadata; current counts are recorded in metadata.total_codes and metadata.current_snapshot.

formal_namespace_promotion_20260319.rationale: Promoted IOM-governing codes from semi-formal abbreviation blocks to formal top-level codebook namespaces while retaining backward compatibility for older scripts.

M_user_groups.note: Section is intentionally reserved for future user-group coding; current release operationalizes age_group_id only.

free_text_fields.operation.signatures[].subtype: Compositional free-text subtype expression; not a controlled vocabulary field.

weak_evidence_translation_guardrail_2026.rule: When linked Evidence is descriptive-only or has significance_id in {SIG_NS, SIG_NA}, IOM performance-effect wording should be hedged rather than asserted as deterministic.

notes: Kawshalya2022_4 was neutralized to DIR_NA rather than hard-switched to DIR_POS, because the generated intervention narrative and linked evidence implied unresolved directional tension.
Historical metadata fields were preserved for backward compatibility; current authoritative snapshot fields remain unchanged.

NUM_NA.name: Numeric Not Reported
NUM_NA.definition: Placeholder indicating that a numeric or numeric-like value is not reported in the source and is intentionally unavailable. Used to standardize missing values across Evidence fields such as relationship.statistical.p_value and relationship.effect_size.ci_lower/ci_upper/threshold.

TXT_NA.name: Text Not Reported
TXT_NA.definition: Placeholder indicating that a text/qualitative field value was not reported or is unavailable. Used for non-ID descriptive fields such as IOM.source_indicator.current_value and IOM.source_indicator.target_value when the source does not specify baseline/target levels.

EFT_RSQ.name: Coefficient of determination (R²)
EFT_RSQ.definition: Proportion of variance explained by a fitted model (e.g., simple linear regression). R² is non-negative and does not carry a direction sign; use direction_id to encode positive/negative trend if needed.

EFT_STD_BETA.name: Standardized regression coefficient (β)
EFT_STD_BETA.definition: Standardized regression coefficient (beta) from a regression model. Sign indicates direction of effect on outcome; magnitude comparable across predictors in the same model.

supporting_papers.citation: Zheng, Y., Lan, S., Zhao, J., Liu, Y., He, S., & Liu, C. (2024). Modeling the Effect of Greenways' Multilevel Visual Characteristics on Thermal Perception in Summer Based on Bayesian Network and Computer Vision. Land, 13(11), 1796.

EFT_IRR.name: Incidence Rate Ratio (IRR)
EFT_IRR.definition: Incidence Rate Ratio (IRR) is the exponentiated coefficient from a count model with a log link (e.g., Poisson or negative binomial regression). It expresses the multiplicative change in the expected incidence/count for a one-unit increase in the predictor (IRR = exp(β)). IRR < 1 indicates a decrease; IRR > 1 indicates an increase.

EFT_COEF.name: Regression coefficient (generic)
EFT_COEF.definition: A regression coefficient reported on the model’s coefficient scale (often unstandardized). Interpretation depends on the model link function (e.g., linear model: change in outcome per unit predictor; log-link count model: log change in expected count).

EFT_REG_COEF.name: Regression coefficient
EFT_REG_COEF.definition: A coefficient from a regression model (typically unstandardized). The sign indicates direction of association; magnitude depends on scaling and the model/link function.

EFT_COR.name: Pearson correlation coefficient (r)
EFT_COR.definition: Pearson correlation coefficient (r) quantifies linear association between two variables. Values range from -1 to 1; the sign indicates direction and the magnitude indicates strength.

EFT_LN_COEF.name: Log-scale coefficient (ln)
EFT_LN_COEF.definition: A coefficient reported on the natural-log scale. Common in generalized linear models with a log link (e.g., Poisson/negative binomial), where exp(coefficient) yields a rate ratio such as IRR.

EFT_MED_PROP.name: Mediation proportion
EFT_MED_PROP.definition: Proportion of the total effect that is explained by an indirect (mediated) pathway in mediation analysis. Typically reported as a proportion between 0 and 1 (or as a percentage).

EFT_NA.name: Missing / not reported
EFT_NA.definition: Placeholder indicating that the effect size type was not reported or not applicable in the source. Use N_missing_values.TXT_NA for missing textual fields.

EFT_RHO.name: Spearman rank correlation coefficient (rho)
EFT_RHO.definition: Rank-based correlation coefficient effect size reported for Spearman correlation analysis. Values range from -1 to 1 and indicate the direction and strength of a monotonic association.
EFT_RHO.note: Used when relationship.statistical.test_type_id = TST_SPR and the reported effect size is a Spearman rho / rank-correlation coefficient.

EFT_INDIRECT.definition: A summary expression for an indirect, mediated, or pathway-specific effect when the source reported mediation results without a fully standardized cross-study metric.

EFT_INTERACT.name: Interaction / moderation summary
EFT_INTERACT.definition: A summary for an interaction, moderation, or conditional effect reported in the source without a directly harmonizable single coefficient.

VAR_SIZ.name: Size
VAR_SIZ.definition: Magnitude, scale, or amount (canopy coverage %, pixel proportion, area)

VAR_SHP.name: Shape
VAR_SHP.definition: Outlines, edges, surfaces defining form (enclosure, canopy silhouette)

VAR_TEX.name: Texture
VAR_TEX.definition: Fine-scale pattern of occluding/non-occluding elements (leaf density, surface roughness)

VAR_COL.name: Colour
VAR_COL.definition: Hue, saturation, brightness in CIE L*a*b* colour space

ACT_ADD.name: Add
ACT_ADD.definition: Introduce a new element where none previously existed

ACT_REM.name: Remove
ACT_REM.definition: Eliminate an existing element from the site

ACT_REP.name: Replace
ACT_REP.definition: Substitute one element type, species, or material for another

ACT_MOD.name: Modify
ACT_MOD.definition: Alter a spatial-visual attribute without changing fundamental type

TER_FG.name: Foreground
TER_FG.definition: Full sensory engagement zone (Gehl, 2010, pp. 33–35; Higuchi, 1983)

TER_MG.name: Middleground
TER_MG.definition: Social field; facial expressions still perceptible (Gehl, 2010; Higuchi, 1983)

OBJ_VEG.name: Vegetation
OBJ_VEG.definition: All plant material from canopy trees to ground cover (Robinson, 2016)

OBJ_BLT.name: Built Structure
OBJ_BLT.definition: Facades, walls, furniture, man-made vertical elements (Simonds & Starke, 2006; Dee, 2001)

OBJ_GRD.name: Ground Surface
OBJ_GRD.definition: Hard and soft base planes (Thompson & Sorvig, 2008)

OBJ_WAT.name: Water
OBJ_WAT.definition: Static, dynamic, and reflective water features

SCP_S1.name: Unit
SCP_S1.definition: Single signature: 1 action × 1 object × 1 tier × 1 variable

SCP_S3.name: Strategic
SCP_S3.definition: Phased programme: multiple operations sequenced over time

OBJ_SKY.reason: Designers cannot physically alter sky; SVF changes are captured by operations on VEG/BLT

ROL_PRI.name: Primary
ROL_PRI.definition: Principal operative component driving the intended indicator change within a multi-signature intervention.

ROL_SEC.name: Secondary
ROL_SEC.definition: Supporting but still material component that co-produces the intended effect alongside the primary signature.

ROL_SUP.name: Supporting
ROL_SUP.definition: Auxiliary component that stabilizes, reinforces, or enriches the main intervention logic without serving as the principal driver.

ROL_TER.name: Tertiary
ROL_TER.definition: Third-order component included when an intervention requires more than two coordinated signatures.

ROL_CTX.name: Contextual
ROL_CTX.definition: Context-setting component that shapes background conditions or framing rather than acting as the direct intervention target.

ROL_MOD.name: Modulating
ROL_MOD.definition: Component that tunes the magnitude, exposure, or perceptual intensity of the main intervention effect.

ROL_COM.name: Complementary
ROL_COM.definition: Component that contributes a compatible parallel benefit while not being strictly required for the core mechanism.

ROL_ENB.name: Enabling
ROL_ENB.definition: Prerequisite component that makes the main intervention feasible or legible.

ROL_ANC.name: Ancillary
ROL_ANC.definition: Minor add-on component with limited direct causal weight but possible co-benefits.

PAT_SUP.name: superposition
PAT_SUP.definition: Two or more signatures are layered onto the same visual field or spatial support so that added material overlays, screens, or co-occupies an existing carrier.

PAT_WEA.name: weaving
PAT_WEA.definition: Multiple signatures are interlaced across the scene, alternating or distributing complementary elements to create a composite visual structure.

PAT_DEF.name: deformation
PAT_DEF.definition: The intervention reshapes an existing element or spatial edge rather than merely adding or removing components.

PAT_RED.name: reduction
PAT_RED.definition: The coordinated logic relies on subtracting or visually down-weighting one set of elements to reveal or strengthen another.

PAT_MON.name: montage
PAT_MON.definition: Distinct signatures are assembled as juxtaposed fragments that collectively alter scene perception through composition.

PAT_COL.name: collage
PAT_COL.definition: Heterogeneous signatures are combined in a patchwork manner while retaining visible differentiation between components.

PAT_PTV.name: phased_temporal_vegetation_layering
PAT_PTV.definition: A strategic sequence in which vegetation layers are introduced or matured over time to progressively reshape the visual field.

Z_abbreviations.signature_axes_order: Operation × Semantic Layer × Spatial Layer × Morphological Layer

ACT_REP.name: Replace
ACT_REP.definition: Substituting one element for another of a different type.

ACT_MOD.name: Modify
ACT_MOD.definition: Adjusting the geometric or visual attributes of an existing element without changing its type.

OBJ_VEG.name: Vegetation
OBJ_VEG.definition: Semantic_layer concept "Vegetation". Used in evidence/iom records to categorize semantic_layer-level attributes; auto-generated definition pending paper-specific elaboration.

OBJ_BLT.name: Built Structure
OBJ_BLT.definition: Semantic_layer concept "Built Structure". Used in evidence/iom records to categorize semantic_layer-level attributes; auto-generated definition pending paper-specific elaboration.

OBJ_GRD.name: Ground Surface
OBJ_GRD.definition: Semantic_layer concept "Ground Surface". Used in evidence/iom records to categorize semantic_layer-level attributes; auto-generated definition pending paper-specific elaboration.

OBJ_WAT.name: Water
OBJ_WAT.definition: Semantic_layer concept "Water". Used in evidence/iom records to categorize semantic_layer-level attributes; auto-generated definition pending paper-specific elaboration.

OBJ_TRE.name: Tree
OBJ_TRE.definition: Object class for trees detected in streetview imagery (parent class for fine-grained tree subtypes such as OST_TRE_TAL = Tall Tree).

OBJ_ART.name: Artificial / Man-made Object
OBJ_ART.definition: Object class for artificial / man-made streetscape objects (fences, signs, poles, railings, etc.) detected in streetview imagery; parent for OST_* subtypes that are human-built infrastructure rather than natural features.

TER_FG.name: Foreground
TER_FG.definition: Spatial_layer concept "Foreground". Used in evidence/iom records to categorize spatial_layer-level attributes; auto-generated definition pending paper-specific elaboration.

TER_MG.name: Middleground
TER_MG.definition: Spatial_layer concept "Middleground". Used in evidence/iom records to categorize spatial_layer-level attributes; auto-generated definition pending paper-specific elaboration.

TER_BG.name: Background
TER_BG.definition: Spatial_layer concept "Background". Used in evidence/iom records to categorize spatial_layer-level attributes; auto-generated definition pending paper-specific elaboration.

VAR_SHP.name: Shape
VAR_SHP.definition: Shape attribute: geometric form of a semantic element including outline, edge characteristics, and spatial configuration of the segmented region.

VAR_SIZ.name: Size
VAR_SIZ.definition: Size attribute: the proportional pixel area or count of a semantic element relative to the total scene. Most pixel-ratio indicators (GVI, SVF, etc.) operate on this attribute.

VAR_POS.name: Position
VAR_POS.definition: Position attribute: the spatial location of a semantic element within the panoramic / perspective image (foreground / middleground / background, or pixel-row / pixel-column position). Distinct from VAR_SIZ (proportion) and VAR_SHP (geometry).

VAR_TEX.name: Texture
VAR_TEX.definition: Texture attribute: pixel-level patterning, granularity, and visual roughness of a semantic element (smooth surfaces vs textured foliage, etc.).

VAR_COL.name: Colour
VAR_COL.definition: Color attribute: chromatic properties of a semantic element including hue, saturation, brightness, color richness, and color diversity.

SCP_S1.name: Unit
SCP_S1.definition: Single signature: 1 action × 1 object × 1 tier × 1 variable

SCP_S3.name: Strategic
SCP_S3.definition: Phased programme: multiple operations sequenced over time

PAT_SUP.name: superposition
PAT_SUP.definition: Two or more signatures are layered onto the same visual field or spatial support so that added material overlays, screens, or co-occupies an existing carrier.

PAT_WEA.name: weaving
PAT_WEA.definition: Multiple signatures are interlaced across the scene, alternating or distributing complementary elements to create a composite visual structure.

PAT_DEF.name: deformation
PAT_DEF.definition: The intervention reshapes an existing element or spatial edge rather than merely adding or removing components.

PAT_RED.name: reduction
PAT_RED.definition: The coordinated logic relies on subtracting or visually down-weighting one set of elements to reveal or strengthen another.

PAT_MON.name: montage
PAT_MON.definition: Distinct signatures are assembled as juxtaposed fragments that collectively alter scene perception through composition.

PAT_COL.name: collage
PAT_COL.definition: Heterogeneous signatures are combined in a patchwork manner while retaining visible differentiation between components.

PAT_PTV.name: phased_temporal_vegetation_layering
PAT_PTV.definition: A strategic sequence in which vegetation layers are introduced or matured over time to progressively reshape the visual field.

ROL_PRI.name: Primary
ROL_PRI.definition: Principal operative component driving the intended indicator change within a multi-signature intervention.

ROL_SEC.name: Secondary
ROL_SEC.definition: Supporting but still material component that co-produces the intended effect alongside the primary signature.

ROL_SUP.name: Supporting
ROL_SUP.definition: Auxiliary component that stabilizes, reinforces, or enriches the main intervention logic without serving as the principal driver.

ROL_TER.name: Tertiary
ROL_TER.definition: Third-order component included when an intervention requires more than two coordinated signatures.

ROL_CTX.name: Contextual
ROL_CTX.definition: Context-setting component that shapes background conditions or framing rather than acting as the direct intervention target.

ROL_MOD.name: Modulating
ROL_MOD.definition: Component that tunes the magnitude, exposure, or perceptual intensity of the main intervention effect.

ROL_COM.name: Complementary
ROL_COM.definition: Component that contributes a compatible parallel benefit while not being strictly required for the core mechanism.

ROL_ENB.name: Enabling
ROL_ENB.definition: Prerequisite component that makes the main intervention feasible or legible.

ROL_ANC.name: Ancillary
ROL_ANC.definition: Minor add-on component with limited direct causal weight but possible co-benefits.
